# Pr 199842 Openai V1 Chat Compat Response Sculpting Stop Coalesce

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_8779ac72d6504759b701178632772f6c
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
v1 OpenAI chat: response sculpting, stop coalescing, and streaming logprob envelope

Motivation: Customers using the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions surface expect a few nuanced behaviors (stop semantics, role-preserving role mapping, streaming logprob envelopes, and deterministic function_call hints). Our compatibility layer has historically handled these piecemeal; this change bundles a deterministic, well-tested mapping layer plus a streaming envelope that reduces parser churn for downstream SDKs.

What this PR does (high level): 1) Introduces a response-sculpting adapter that normalizes incoming v1 parameters (temperature/top_p/stop arrays/stop strings) into Redwood runtime configs, coalescing stop tokens with deduplication and priority ordering (explicit stop > system-supplied stop). 2) Adds a streaming logprob envelope: each delta event includes a compact logprob map when requested (logprobs mode) and a stable "terminate_reason" token on final events to remove ambiguity for partial consumers. 3) Preserves role metadata and adds role_alias resolution so tools that pass custom roles still receive canonical role labels in responses. 4) Extends function_call handling to surface both suggested and forced calls: when the model suggests a call we emit a standardized tool-call event; when callers force a function_call we validate against registered schemas and return an immediate structured output. 5) Implements conservative backpressure hooks in the stream coalescer to improve stability under bursty clients.

Compatibility notes: The adapter keeps backward-compatible defaults; previous consumers that do not request logprobs or function schemas should see no change. New behavior is opt-in via the v1 parameter mapping and an additional header (X-OpenAI-Compat: true) for private deployments that want explicit parity checks.

Testing and validation: Unit tests added for stop coalescing, role aliasing, function schema validation and negative cases. End-to-end tests simulate streaming clients (chunk reordering, reconnect resume) and assert final assembled events for both SDKs. Benchmarked against a production-like workload: median p99 tail latency increased by ~1ms in the worst case; CPU overhead on the agent path is <2% for common workloads.

Rollout plan: Feature gated behind openai_v1_compat flag. Canary on dedicated region us-west-2 for 48 hours with traffic mirroring; if stable, promote to main with a one-week monitoring window (alert on increased stream reconnects or event schema validation failures).

Related: ENG-1842 (param mapping), SUP-5523 (customer incident that exposed stop token mismatch).
Tomás Rivera: Important to ensure ordering for coalesced stops — what happens when a client supplies overlapping regex-based stops? Please add a test.
Asha Kapoor: Added deterministic precedence and a unit test for overlapping regex stops (stop_coalesce_test.go). Regex stops are treated as last-resort matches and are only applied when exact tokens don't trigger earlier stop rules.
Ling Chen: Streaming logprob envelope looks good but please document the size impact on the stream frame and add opt-out for clients concerned about bandwidth.
Asha Kapoor: Introduced a `logprobs=none|summary|full` mapping; default remains none. Documented bandwidth considerations in docs/openai_compat.md.
Noah Patel: Function schema validation makes sense; can we fail closed for invalid schemas or return a 4xx error to the caller?
Asha Kapoor: We return a 422 with a structured error payload and a telemetry event. This is covered in schema_validator.go and e2e tests.
CI: flaky test in e2e on reconnect simulated network delay — I retriggered and it passed on re-run.
Add OpenAI /v1 chat compatibility adapter: normalized params (stop coalescing & dedupe), streaming logprob envelope with terminate_reason, role alias resolution, and deterministic function_call schema validation. Opt-in via openai_v1_compat feature flag and X-OpenAI-Compat header.
